sections.name: I. Marvel — Disney+
sections.desc: WandaVision, Loki, Hawkeye, Moon Knight, X-Men 97, etc.

items.title: WandaVision
items.pitch: Phase Four opener. Wanda and Vision live through a sequence of TV-history pastiches — 50s sitcom, 70s family comedy, 90s mockumentary — while her grief metastasizes. Best-received Marvel D+ show by most lights.

items.title: Loki
items.pitch: Loki vs. the bureaucratic TVA, vs. variants of himself, vs. Kang. Two seasons that arguably set up the entire next phase of the MCU.

items.title: X-Men '97
items.pitch: Direct continuation of the 90s animated series. Picks up after Xavier's apparent death; surprised everyone by being legitimately great.

sections.name: II. Marvel — Netflix
sections.desc: Daredevil, Jessica Jones, Luke Cage, Iron Fist, Punisher, Defenders.

items.title: Daredevil
items.pitch: Charlie Cox as Matt Murdock. Hallway fight scenes, Vincent D'Onofrio's Wilson Fisk, the gold standard for street-level Marvel TV.

sections.name: III. DC
sections.desc: Smallville, Arrow, Flash, Gotham, Watchmen HBO, Peacemaker, Titans.

sections.name: IV. Animated
sections.desc: X-Men: The Animated Series, Batman: TAS, Justice League Unlimited, Spider-Verse animation.

items.title: Batman: The Animated Series
items.pitch: Bruce Timm/Paul Dini's noir-coded Batman. Mark Hamill as Joker, Kevin Conroy as Bruce. Defining versions for many.

sections.name: V. Deconstructive
sections.desc: The Boys, Invincible, Watchmen HBO — the genre questioning itself.

items.title: The Boys
items.pitch: Garth Ennis adaptation. Supes are corporate-controlled celebrities; Homelander is what happens when Superman has unmet emotional needs. Goes harder than the comics in places.

items.title: Invincible
items.pitch: Animated Robert Kirkman adaptation. Pulls a brutal twist in the pilot that reframes the entire genre. Surprisingly violent for animation, surprisingly emotional.

items.title: Watchmen
items.pitch: Damon Lindelof's HBO sequel-not-sequel to the Moore/Gibbons graphic novel. Set in modern-day Tulsa, anchored to the 1921 race massacre. The genre done well.

sections.name: VI. Indie / Other
sections.desc: Preacher, The Umbrella Academy, The Tick, etc.
